notes:
Works Great after a few tweaks. Performance in battles and on campainmap is almost on par with Windows.

Necessary Fixes:
Right click game i library -> properties -> betas -> select launcher_openbeta. 

Other fixes:
You need to start the game and it will create necessary config files.

Remove battery icon: 
Change  "batter_meter false" in file :
"steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts/preferences.script.txt"

Mods: 
To enable mods just place mod .pack file in "Total War Rome II/data/"

To activate mod create file:
"steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts/user.script.txt"

And in user.script.txt write this if you want to use Divide et imperia for example:
mod "_divide_et_impera_release_12_Part1.pack";
mod "_divide_et_impera_release_12_Part2.pack";
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Launcher renders incorrectly, could not test the game
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: I followed the instructions on some other reports to remove the battery indicator. Set graphics to the "High" Preset and change the Shader Model to 4.1 instead of 5. No way I could find of getting around the white screened launcher but it isnt a big deal honestly. Had no crashes, about 45 fps on campaign map and 70 in battle.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
Overall it works perfectly. Eventhough, I have an issue. 

Shadows are bugged, darker than they should be. I'll leave a link so you can see the problem, if anyone knows any solutions, please report them.

http://tinypic.com/view.php?pic=57kmb&s=5

In other aspects, the game runs nicely. I've had no crashes in sea battles, no crashes in high settings, only a few crashes in the beginning of a battle, but it's almost residual. 

Mods are inoperative via steam workshop. Some testers have posted solutions to make them work without using steam workshop.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Seems to work quite well after following options setup: Set shadow graphic quality to low, because shadows are almost black otherwise. Also disabled cloud shadows; Set water quality to high; Everything else is Ultra;  Launcher is white, but game launches if I click to correct place;  Sometimes game fails to exit properly, which I can fix by running `killall launcher.exe`

Another issue is that I have a battery in the top of my screen that overlays the game but not the faction circles when processing a turn. This might be related to the use of a wireless mouse. Game is definitely very playable.

title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: First, graphics were looking horrible (DX9 fallback?). Installation of mesa-vulkan-drivers:i386 package fixed the problem. Battery symbol can be removed by editing steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts/preferences.script.txt (search for "battery"). Works perfectly now, on very high details (textures and water ultra).
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
I am playing the campaign and works perfectly fine for me.
In the beginning I had two problems: ______ 1. Game had some crashes at the beginning of battles. __ Decreasing the video quality settings fixed this for me. ______ 2. A battery icon was displayed at the top of the screen. __ As described in an earlier report, this can be fixed by setting "batter_meter false" (line 55) in the file : ".steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts/preferences.script.txt".
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
gfx_aa 0 in the config is the key to success. It is the first AA setting on the left side in the in game graphic settings. 
gfx_texture_filtering (the last AA Setting on the right) is no problem.
I can play with all in Ultra, Shader 5.0, Texturfilter 8x no probs at all. But gfx_aa should disabled otherwise you get crashes, texture glitches (dark textures) etc. 
Forget the Benchmark Test. Run a true battle or the campaign for real tests. I got everytime dark textures in the benchmark test and if I run a normal game, everything is perfect ... Played more than a week with Proton 4.2 on this settings. 
No issues
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box, no tweaks required, no noticeable performance hit

notes: Works, but shadows are black instead of shadows.  Turning off shadows and some may think they are seeing normal color, but what you are really seeing is all the colors are just off.  Comparing this to Windows and it is then plainly obvious that none of the color pallets are accurate while running under Linux.  Is it playable?  Yes.  Is it enjoyable?  and that is a big no.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition
